thoughts.en: Django provides powerful ORM to query and modify database. Some built-in apps are written using the ORM. (probably in many third-party apps too). For example, it allows quick setup of the admin panel, authentication and migrations.
thoughts.uk: Django provides powerful ORM to query and modify database. Some built-in apps are written using the ORM. (probably in many third-party apps too). For example, it allows quick setup of the admin panel, authentication and migrations.

thoughts.en: ORM covers: simple CRUD commands, setting up default query behavior (filtering, ordering), 1-1, many-1, many-many relations, aggregations/annotations, support for raw SQL queries, transactions and savepoints, use of tablespaces (not creation), fixtures, multi-DB design.
thoughts.uk: ORM covers: simple CRUD commands, setting up default query behavior (filtering, ordering), 1-1, many-1, many-many relations, aggregations/annotations, support for raw SQL queries, transactions and savepoints, use of tablespaces (not creation), fixtures, multi-DB design.

thoughts.en: Important terms of the ORM: Model, Model instance methods, Meta options (abstract, app_label, base_manager_name, db_table, db_table_comment, db_tablespace, default_manager_name, default_related_name, get_latest_by, managed, order_with_respect_to, ordering, permissions, default_permissions, proxy, required_db_features, required_db_vendor, select_on_save, indexes, unique_together, constraints, verbose_name, verbose_name_plural, label, label_lower), Field, Relationship Field (ForeignKey, ManyToManyField, OneToOneField), Manager, Manager methods, QuerySet, Field lookups (prebuilt: exact, iexact, contains, icontains, in, gt, gte, lt, lte, startswith, istartswith, endswith, iendswith, range, date, year, iso_year, month, day, week, week_day, iso_week_day, quarter, time, hour, minute, second, isnull, regex, iregex), Query-related tools (Q, Prefetch, prefetch_related_objects, FilteredRelation), Aggregation functions (expressions, output_field, filter, default, **extra, AnyValue, Avg, Count, Max, Min, StdDev, Sum, Variance, StringAgg), Query Expressions, Conditional Expressions, Database Functions, Index, Constraint.
thoughts.uk: Important terms of the ORM: Model, Model instance methods, Meta options (abstract, app_label, base_manager_name, db_table, db_table_comment, db_tablespace, default_manager_name, default_related_name, get_latest_by, managed, order_with_respect_to, ordering, permissions, default_permissions, proxy, required_db_features, required_db_vendor, select_on_save, indexes, unique_together, constraints, verbose_name, verbose_name_plural, label, label_lower), Field, Relationship Field (ForeignKey, ManyToManyField, OneToOneField), Manager, Manager methods, QuerySet, Field lookups (prebuilt: exact, iexact, contains, icontains, in, gt, gte, lt, lte, startswith, istartswith, endswith, iendswith, range, date, year, iso_year, month, day, week, week_day, iso_week_day, quarter, time, hour, minute, second, isnull, regex, iregex), Query-related tools (Q, Prefetch, prefetch_related_objects, FilteredRelation), Aggregation functions (expressions, output_field, filter, default, **extra, AnyValue, Avg, Count, Max, Min, StdDev, Sum, Variance, StringAgg), Query Expressions, Conditional Expressions, Database Functions, Index, Constraint.

thoughts.en: It is important to profile queries since it is not always obvious how many times DB is called or how large is the cache. Take a look at QuerySet.explain(), connection.execute_wrapper(), django-debug-toolbar.
thoughts.uk: It is important to profile queries since it is not always obvious how many times DB is called or how large is the cache. Take a look at QuerySet.explain(), connection.execute_wrapper(), django-debug-toolbar.

thoughts.en: Note that some methods bypass model methods and hooks by running SQL statement instead.
thoughts.uk: Note that some methods bypass model methods and hooks by running SQL statement instead.

thoughts.en: It’s difficult to intuit how the ORM will translate complex querysets into SQL queries so when in doubt, inspect the SQL with str(queryset.query) and write plenty of tests.
thoughts.uk: It’s difficult to intuit how the ORM will translate complex querysets into SQL queries so when in doubt, inspect the SQL with str(queryset.query) and write plenty of tests.

thoughts.en: Django does many things automatically, for example it provides names for aggregations, setups primary keys, chooses DB connection, etc. This behavior can be overridden.
thoughts.uk: Django does many things automatically, for example it provides names for aggregations, setups primary keys, chooses DB connection, etc. This behavior can be overridden.

thoughts.en: Some provided functionality is available not on all DBs. Check support for your DB. For example there is the support of complex querying options for postgres only.
thoughts.uk: Some provided functionality is available not on all DBs. Check support for your DB. For example there is the support of complex querying options for postgres only.

thoughts.en: Django allows setting up table level functionality (using Managers) and instance level functionality (using Models).
thoughts.uk: Django allows setting up table level functionality (using Managers) and instance level functionality (using Models).

thoughts.en: Django has functionality for generating fixtures to pre-populate the database with data for tests or to provide some initial data using the loaddata command.
thoughts.uk: Django has functionality for generating fixtures to pre-populate the database with data for tests or to provide some initial data using the loaddata command.
